events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON. Need compute tension in each sling leg. Image: two-leg sling arrangement, load 6000 lb, horizontal sling angle at load 25 degrees. Each leg tagged WLL 7000 lb vertical rating. Need compute tension per leg. For symmetric two-leg sling, each leg tension T = (Load/2) / sin(theta) where theta is angle from horizontal? Let's parse. Horizontal sling angle shown at load is 25°. Typically sling angle is measured from horizontal. Vertical component per leg = T * sin(theta). Sum vertical components = Load. So T = Load/(2 sin(theta)) = 6000/(2 sin25°). sin25°=0.422618. denominator=0.845236. T=7099.6 lb ~7100 lb. If angle measured from vertical? But text says horizontal sling angle shown at load, and diagram shows angle between sling and horizontal dashed line =25°. So yes from horizontal. Tension exceeds WLL 7000 lb. Also angle less than 30° from horizontal is generally prohibited or requires special consideration; sling tension increases dramatically. ASME B30.9 and OSHA require sling angle considerations; rated at vertical, capacity decreases with angle. Here actual tension ~7100 > 7000, fail. Need output JSON with fields. decision fail, risk high/critical? Tension just slightly over WLL and angle 25°, maybe high. component_conditions none? Could list non-compliant due to sling angle/tension exceeding WLL. The schema component_conditions categories: installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Use non-compliant. percent_complete 0 planned. value 7100 (pounds). confidence 1.0. findings: sling leg tension exceeds tagged WLL; sling angle 25° from horizontal is below common 30° minimum. actions: increase sling angle/use longer slings or four-leg arrangement/reduce load/select higher WLL slings; do not lift until configuration engineered within ratings. references: ASME B30.9, OSHA 1926.251. Need ensure JSON valid.
